headline: Investors see Micron earnings as pulse check of AI rally momentum
summary: Investors are seeking signs that the U.S. stock market rally fueled by artificial intelligence has more life left in it, and the upcoming Micron Technology earnings will check the pulse of chip demand ‌to see if it is still accelerating.  Despite a sharp mid-week selloff, major U.S. stock indexes are hovering near all-time highs, supported ‌by robust corporate earnings driven by an AI investment boom and relief from the Iran war.  Micron's shares are up 298% this year, and the memory chip maker's quarterly report ​on Wednesday, June 24, will help investors gauge whether the surge in spending on data centers and the resulting profits generated across the semiconductor sector can continue to surprise to the upside.

raw.headline: Investors see Micron earnings as pulse check of AI rally momentum

Micron's shares are up 298% this year, and the memory chip maker's quarterly report ​on Wednesday, June 24, will help investors gauge whether the surge in spending on data centers and the resulting profits generated across the semiconductor sector can continue to surprise to the upside.

headline: Apple's Price Warning Sends Memory Chips To A Record, Accenture Cracks 18%
summary: Tim Cook's warning that iPhone and Mac prices are going up because memory chips have become too expensive lit a fire under the storage names. Micron printed a record $1,133. Intel added 10% on the back of the Trump stake math. Accenture took an 18% beating on weak consulting bookings. Vance's cancelled trip to Switzerland nudged oil back above $80.

headline: Micron’s Sanjay Mehrotra Saw the Memory Bust. Now He’s Leading the Memory Boom.
summary: Micron Technology  CEO Sanjay Mehrotra, 67, is no stranger to the boom/bust cycle in memory chips.  Mehrotra, who took the helm in 2017, appears to have learned from the downturn.  Under his stewardship, Micron exited the cyclical consumer business and focused laser-like on enterprise-grade memory for data centers.
